title: Antillanthus leucolepis
intro: Antillanthus leucolepis, also known as Leucolepis, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of South America, belonging to the family Asteraceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and combating infections. The bioactive constituents of Antillanthus leucolepis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
